_source: Kurczak & Fish, Art & Science of Vedic Astrology Vol I, Ch.12 'The Vimshottari Dasha System' + Ch.13 'Transits'.
_note: The TIMING layer — WHEN a chart's promises fire. Velea already COMPUTES dashas & transits; this canon is the READING layer (the law that dasha+transit are timing tools that never contradict the natal promise, plus the interpretive rules). This is the knot-firing bridge: a knot (life-area/karaka lit natally) manifests when its dasha/bhukti runs AND its significators are transited.

vimshottari._desc: The 120-year Vimshottari mahadasha cycle. Sequence is fixed; the starting point is set by the natal Moon's nakshatra.
vimshottari.bhuktiStructure: Each mahadasha divides into 9 bhuktis (sub-periods) that run in the SAME sequence order, ALWAYS starting with the dasha lord itself (Rahu dasha → Rahu bhukti, then Jupiter, Saturn…). Bhuktis subdivide further into antardashas (same rule). Bhukti length is proportional to both planets' dasha years.
vimshottari.readingLaw: "Whatever has been described by way of planetary effects will occur in the planet's [maha]dasha, in its bhukti and in its antardasha." — Phaldeepika 21:1. A planet delivers its natal promise (its significations, the houses it owns/occupies, the yogas it forms) during ITS periods. A yoga/knot that never gets its planets' dasha in the relevant life-stage stays latent.

transits._desc: Gochara — the current sky read against the natal chart. Activates, it does not rewrite: a transit COLORS the natal planet/house it contacts; the natal planet never modifies the transiting planet.
transits.referenceFrame: This text reads transits PRIMARILY from the Ascendant (lagna), holding the Moon-sign reference in a subordinate role — it finds the classical Moon-only method less reliable. (Relevant to Velea: favour the ascendant frame; use Chandra lagna as a secondary lens.)
transits.cannotContradictNatal: Transits (like dashas) NEVER contradict the birth chart's basic indications — they only time when a natal potential manifests. Predicting off a transit against the natal grain is the classic beginner error.
transits.strongestContact: A transiting planet's effect peaks at conjunction or opposition to a natal planet, strongest within 1° of exactitude and declining once separated by 1°. Retrograde adjusts the reading; a STATIONARY planet (at its direct/retro turn) hits hardest.
transits.weightBySpeed: Slow planets (Jupiter, Saturn) carry the long-term story and form drishti aspects on transit (as does Mars). Fast planets (Moon, Sun, Venus, Mercury) matter mainly at exact conjunction to a natal planet and for only days (Moon: hours — except a Full/New Moon conjunction, up to a week). Outer planets (Uranus/Neptune/Pluto) count ONLY at close conjunction/opposition/square to a natal planet — disregard their sign/house transits.
transits.quickScan: For a fast read of current influences, focus on the house positions and aspects of transiting Jupiter and Saturn — basic but practical and effective.
transits.coordinateWithDasha: The single most important rule: coordinate transits with the running dasha/bhukti. In Jupiter dasha–Venus bhukti, the transits of Jupiter and Venus (even the ordinarily-minor house/sign Venus transits) gain weight, because the bhukti's results depend heavily on the transit strength of the bhukti lord. But transits do NOT override the dasha's indications.
transits.receptivity: Weak natal planets are hurt by strong malefic transits and can't fully receive benefic transits. Strong natal benefics resist malefic transits; strong natal malefics resist benefic transits. A weak planet in its bhukti can't well protect the houses it rules regardless of its transit strength.

perPlanet.Sun/Moon: Use together — only the Full and New Moon phases matter. Eclipses amplify: a SOLAR eclipse (New Moon) > New Moon → dramatic new developments on the house/planet touched; a LUNAR eclipse amplifies the Full Moon. An eclipse conjunct any natal planet is ominous/disruptive (the Nodes are there); it also affects the house it falls in.
perPlanet.Mercury: Rarely major — communications, meetings, journeys, undertakings; retrograde delays them.
perPlanet.Venus: Harmonious, pleasant experiences; meetings with loved ones.
perPlanet.Mars: Urge to action; disrupts established rhythm; energizes the planet/house it passes or aspects.
perPlanet.Jupiter: Urge to expand into new experience; attunes to future possibility; abundance/good fortune (can over-inflate).
perPlanet.Saturn: Stabilizes, delays, slows, constricts the planet it contacts and the house it passes through.
perPlanet.Rahu: Desires and obsessions tied to the planet/house it contacts.
perPlanet.Ketu: Awakens fears, phobias, obsessions — or a spiritually uplifting influence.

sadeSati._desc: The ~7½-year period of Saturn transiting the natal Moon sign plus the signs on either side.
sadeSati.definition: Sade Sati = 'seven and a half' — Saturn (2½ years per sign) crossing the Moon sign and both adjacent signs. Lowers energy, slows life's pace, dampens emotional buoyancy, darkens outlook.
sadeSati.veleaStance: The book flags Sade Sati as folk lore ABSENT from the classical texts, and advises caution about over-weighting it (and Moon-referenced transits generally) in a modern Western practice. Practical rule it does keep: the impact is real mainly within 12° of an exact Saturn–Moon conjunction; outside that, it can be largely ignored.

maturityOfPlanets._desc: The AGE at which each planet 'matures' — becomes truly functional. From that year, the houses it OWNS and the house it SITS in grow more pronounced for ~12 months; most noticeable for the ruling planet and the chart's strongest planet. Fires independently of dasha/transit.
maturityOfPlanets._note: Book states each as a span (Jupiter 15→16, Sun 21→22, Moon 23→24, Venus 24→25, Mars 27→28, Mercury 31→32, Saturn 35→36, Rahu 41→42, Ketu 47→48); ageYears above is the completed year (the end of each span).

planetaryAges._desc: The life-PERIOD each planet forms a background influence over. OVERRIDDEN by dashas, transits, and the maturity years above — best used when a chart has no dasha/transit info.
planetaryAges._note: Example (book): if Venus is poorly placed but the Sun is well placed, success/happiness is unlikely until age 23 (when the Sun's period opens). A weak Mars + strong Jupiter → lack of success/initiative until the late 50s (the Jupiter period).
